content.problemStatement:
A set SSS is said to be good iff :


For all x,y,z∈Sx, y, z \in Sx,y,z∈S, such that x≠y,x≠z,y≠zx \ne y, x \ne z, y \ne zx=y,x=z,y=z; (x,y,z)(x, y, z)(x,y,z) are the side lengths of a valid non-degenerate triangle.
Count the number of sets SSS of NNN distinct integers which are good, and all elements of the set SiS_iSi​ satisfy 1≤Si≤M1 \le S_i \le M1≤Si​≤M.

Since the answer may be large, output it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the size of the set SSS, and the upper bound on the elements of set SSS.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, a single integer denoting the number of good sets modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of MMM does not exceed 10610^6106 over all test cases.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111 : The only valid set is S={2,3,4}S = \{2, 3, 4\}S={2,3,4}. {1,3,4}\{1, 3, 4\}{1,3,4} is invalid as there doesn't exist a triangle with side lengths 1,3,1, 3,1,3, and 444.
